Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Louis Cherry To Respond To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3277 Against Defendant Louis Cherry Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Declaration Of Charles Pressman Proposed Order
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RAMESES JOHNSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT LOUIS CHERRY TO RESPOND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,277 AGAINST DEFENDANT LOUIS CHERRY

Appearances: Charles A. Pressman of David H. Pierce & Associates PC (818.826.5480) for plaintiff and moving party; Sydney Jay Hall of the Law Office of Sydney Jay Hall (650.342.1830) for defendant Louis Cherry

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion of plaintiff Rameses Johnson for responses to form interrogatories, set one, is granted. Johnson shows good cause; the discovery was served and defendant Louis Cherry has not responded. Defendant is ordered to serve complete, objection-free, verified responses by February 20, 2026. (CCP 2030.290(a) [waiver of objections by untimely response].) Plaintiff shall give notice.

Plaintiff seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where the party does not oppose the motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders defendant to pay $2000 in sanctions to plaintiff by March 13, 2026. The court reduces the amount in the exercise of its discretion and because no reply brief was required. Order signed. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant American Precision Builders To Respond To Form D4terrogatories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,277 Against Defendant American Precision Builders; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Charles Pressman; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RAMESES JOHNSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT AMERICAN PRECISION BUILDERS TO RESPOND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,277 AGAINST DEFENDANT AMERICAN PRECISION BUILDERS

Appearances: Charles A. Pressman of David H. Pierce & Associates PC (818.826.5480) for plaintiff and moving party; Sydney Jay Hall of the Law Office of Sydney Jay Hall (650.342.1830) for defendant Louis Cherry

Discussed. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion of plaintiff Rameses Johnson for responses to form interrogatories, set one, is granted. Johnson shows good cause; the discovery was served and defendant American Precision Builders has not responded. Defendant is ordered to serve complete, objection-free, verified responses by February 20, 2026. (CCP 2030.290(a) [waiver of objections by untimely response].) Plaintiff shall give notice.

Plaintiff seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where the party does not oppose the motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders defendant to pay $2000 in sanctions to plaintiff by March 13, 2026. The court reduces the amount in the exercise of its discretion and because no reply brief was required. Counsel are directed to meet and confer as to the status of the entity American Precision Builders. Order signed. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
